question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network string value containing the NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER '-' (e.g., 'mainnet-evil') so that getNetwork() returns 'mainnet', causing RecalculatePendingRewardMigration.doMigrate() to execute the pending reward recalculation SQL against a non-mainnet dataset, leading to incorrect pending_reward values being written to entity_stake and theft of staking rewards from legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getNetwork() strips everything after '-' and returns lowercase, and exploit this by injecting a network value like 'MAINNET-suffix' to bypass network-identity checks in FixStakedBeforeEnabledMigration, causing the migration to run on a non-mainnet node and zero out legitimate pending_reward and staked_node_id_start values, effectively stealing accumulated staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network string with a crafted prefix (e.g., 'other-mainnet') so that getNetworkPrefix() returns 'mainnet' while getNetwork() returns 'other', causing the importer to skip the RecalculatePendingRewardMigration (which only runs for mainnet/testnet) while routing to mainnet cloud buckets, resulting in stale or zero pending_reward values for all staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where getNetwork() returns an empty string (by setting network to '-suffix') causing FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP.get(hederaNetwork) to return null in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration, silently skipping the pending reward recalculation and leaving all entity_stake.pending_reward values at zero, denying stakers their earned rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: ConsensusMode (EQUAL)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ConsensusMode.EQUAL setting (all nodes equally weighted) to cause the importer to accept a crafted stream file signed by a minority of low-stake nodes, where that file contains fraudulent NodeStakeUpdate transactions with inflated reward_rate values, leading to over-calculation of pending_reward in entity_stake and theft of staking rewards from account 0.0.800?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: ConsensusMode (STAKE)] Can an unprivileged external user, under ConsensusMode.STAKE where all nodes specify their own stake, exploit the absence of address-book filtering to inject a rogue node with artificially high stake weight, causing the importer to accept stream files from that rogue node containing manipulated staking_reward_transfer records, resulting in fraudulent pending_reward accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: ConsensusMode (STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK)] Can an unprivileged external user, when consensusMode is STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK, exploit the mismatch warning path (addressBook.getNodeCount() != nodeStakes.size()) to cause the importer to fall back to address-book node count for consensus weight calculation, reducing the effective quorum threshold and allowing acceptance of stream files with manipulated staking reward data signed by fewer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: initialAddressBook] Can an unprivileged external user, by influencing the initialAddressBook path to point to a crafted protobuf address book file containing attacker-controlled nodes, cause AddressBookServiceImpl to load that address book as the bootstrap, so that under STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK mode only attacker nodes are used for consensus weight, enabling acceptance of fraudulent stream files with manipulated staking reward transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodePublicKey override (which replaces all address book entry public keys with a single key) to cause the importer to verify all stream file signatures against one attacker-controlled key, allowing the attacker to forge stream files containing fraudulent staking_reward_transfer records and inflate pending_reward for attacker-controlled accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: startDate] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the startDate property to a value after the FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP for mainnet (1666310400447390002 ns), causing the DateRangeFilter to exclude all NodeStakeUpdate transactions from before that date, resulting in missing node_stake rows and a zero or incorrect pending_reward calculation for all staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: endDate] Can an unprivileged external user set endDate to a timestamp just before a NodeStakeUpdate transaction, causing the importer to stop processing before that staking period's reward_rate is recorded in node_stake, so that EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() finds no new epoch_day and leaves pending_reward stale, denying stakers their earned rewards for that period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: startDate] Can an unprivileged external user set startDate to a value after HistoricalAccountInfoMigration.EXPORT_DATE (2019-09-14T00:00:10Z), causing the historical account info migration to be skipped, so that pre-genesis staking account metadata (staked_node_id, stake_period_start) is never populated, resulting in zero pending_reward for all accounts that staked before open access?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: migration (CaseInsensitiveMap)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CaseInsensitiveMap for migration properties to inject a migration entry with a case-variant key (e.g., 'RecalculatePendingReward' vs 'recalculatependingreward') that disables the RecalculatePendingRewardMigration, causing the pending reward recalculation to be skipped and leaving entity_stake.pending_reward values incorrectly accumulated (double-counted), enabling theft of staking rewards?
